quotes.text: Daniel shares lessons from making his new game, RetroRapid!, more accessible, (...) customizable settings to make gameplay inclusive.
It’s funny how someone that I see as an accessibility expert mentions “learn from actual experts”. Daniel (...) describes his first approach to making his game more accessible.
I expected little side-stories about how other games get accessibility wrong. Instead, I enjoyed reading an entirely positive post about how to do it right.
If you haven't seen it yet, please add The Advent of iOS Accessibility (...) to your list. It's really well done from someone who both knows what he's talking about and is excellent at explaining it.

posts.heading: This post was featured and referenced in

mentions.title: Peter Heery: Supporting VoiceOver typing preferences

quotes.text: I struggled to decide whether this article (...) should live in the code section or the design section. It straddles the divide, but either way, it’s worth a read.
As noted by Dani Devesa (...) adding the `keyboardKey` trait to a `UIButton` will mean it is no longer announced as 'button' by VoiceOver.
quotes.sourceTitle: Peter Heery: Supporting VoiceOver typing preferences
